description: Implement `hex inference q-report` per ADR-2604151300. Reads rl_q_entry + rl_experience tables from SpacetimeDB via a new hex-nexus REST endpoint and renders a tier/model/Q/visits/trend table. P1 builds the nexus endpoint, P2 wires the CLI subcommand + renderers, P3 adds the --watch streaming path, P4 adds the doc + smoke test.

tasks.title: Add QReportEntry { state, model, q_value, visits, last_seen, trend_7d } struct in hex-core/src/inference_q.rs with serde derives + serde_json round-trip test.

Add hex-nexus route GET /api/inference/q-report with query params tier, task_type, model, since (duration), limit (default 50), sort (q|visits|recency, default visits). Subscribe to rl_q_entry + rl_experience via existing STDB client; compute trend_7d by diffing current Q vs Q 7d ago from rl_experience reward sequence.
Integration test: hit /api/inference/q-report against an in-memory STDB fixture seeded with 5 q-entries + 10 experiences, assert correct filtering, sorting, and trend computation.

Add QReport variant to InferenceSubCmd enum in hex-cli/src/commands/inference.rs with clap args: --tier, --task-type, --model, --sort (default visits), --limit (default 20), --format (table|json|yaml, default table), --since (duration), --watch (bool).
Implement non-watch handler: hits hex-nexus GET /api/inference/q-report with the user's flags as query string, deserializes Vec<QReportEntry>, dispatches to renderer based on --format.

Table renderer: aligned columns STATE, MODEL, Q (signed, 4dp), VISITS, LAST_SEEN (relative duration), TREND (↑/→/↓ from trend_7d threshold ±0.05). Footer: 3 lines — convergence, drift, coverage — computed from the result set.
JSON + YAML renderers: pass-through serde_json::to_string_pretty / serde_yaml::to_string of the Vec<QReportEntry>.

Unit tests: table renderer formats fixture data correctly (golden test under tests/golden/q-report-default.txt); JSON renderer round-trips; trend symbol selection at boundary values (-0.05, 0.0, +0.05).
Add hex-nexus route GET /api/inference/q-report/stream as Server-Sent Events. Push a fresh report whenever rl_q_entry mutates (subscribe via STDB row-update callback).
CLI --watch mode: open SSE stream to /api/inference/q-report/stream, repaint table on each event using a small ANSI cursor-home + clear-below trick. Ctrl+C closes the stream cleanly.

Update docs/INFERENCE.md: replace the static 'after 3 pipeline runs' Q-table example with a `hex inference q-report` invocation showing live data. Add a 'Reading the Q-report' subsection (5-10 lines) explaining trend, convergence, drift, coverage.

Workspace build gate + binary rebuild: cargo check --workspace, cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast, then cargo build -p hex-cli --release && cargo build -p hex-nexus --release. Smoke: `hex inference q-report --limit 5` returns within 2s without panic on a clean install with no Q-data (empty table is a valid state, must render gracefully).

Wire the Q-report endpoint into the idle-research swarm performance analyst (ADR-2604151200 P3.1) as one of its inputs — flag any model with trend ↓ AND visits ≥ 5 as a Finding with severity=med, suggested_action=adr.
